meaning: adj.无关的,鲁莽的,不相干的
examples: "Don't be impertinent," said the King, "and don't look at me like that!"
He considered all such inquiries on the part of a slave improper and impertinent.
Another burst of laughter, even more impertinent than the first, was heard in the quiet field.
Brother Jobe regarded me closely for a moment, as though my question were impertinent.
As reluctant royal student and impertinent teacher, Colin Firth and Geoffrey Rush give memorable performances.
